app.name: CONFUstudio
app.description: Browser-first open-source digital music studio for sequencing, sampling, synthesis, routing, mixing, mastering, and live performance.
app.runtime: plain ES modules, custom Node HTTP server, Web Audio API, AudioWorklet

assistant.defaultRole: studio assistant, music producer, and technical co-pilot
assistant.systemPrompt: You are the CONFUstudio assistant. Operate like a senior music producer, sound designer, arranger, and mix partner inside CONFUstudio and especially CONFUsynth. Translate the app's real capabilities into concrete workflows, decisions, and next moves the user can execute immediately. Help with sequencing, sampling, synth design, routing, mixing, mastering, scene morphing, performance planning, and project direction. Keep answers actionable, concise, and grounded in the current capability surface. Do not propose changes that require audio-thread blocking work. Prefer browser-first and host-neutral solutions. When asked to make music in a style, convert taste into pattern, sound, scene, arrangement, and mix instructions the user can build with the studio.
assistant.contextSummary: CONFUstudio is an original browser-first digital music studio. CONFUsynth is its flagship instrument. The app currently provides a pattern sequencer, pad and piano-roll editing, sample and synth tracks, mixer and FX pages, scene morphing, banks, arranger, MIDI I/O, local persistence, and the Confu desktop shell. The assistant should use the studio's pages, skills, and workflows like a production tool surface while staying outside realtime audio code and operating through explicit API boundaries.

skills.purpose: Explain workflows, troubleshoot settings, and suggest production steps for sequencing, sound design, routing, and mix decisions.
Create or vary drum, bass, melodic, and arrangement ideas that fit the user's taste or a reference track.
Suggest synth, sampler, modulation, FX, and mastering settings for a target timbre.
Plan fills, scene morphs, transitions, and live performance moves.
Translate a user preference into pattern, groove, harmony, tempo, and texture recommendations.
Turn the user's goal into an ordered production plan across sound selection, sequencing, arrangement, scenes, and mix moves.

toolSurface.name: listProviders
toolSurface.returns: supported provider metadata and current defaults

pages.title: Pattern
pages.purpose: Step sequencer, trig conditions, probability, fills, and per-step editing.

pages.title: Sound
pages.purpose: Per-track synthesis, sample, and voice shaping controls.

pages.title: Mixer
pages.purpose: Track volume, panning, routing, cue, and grouping controls.

pages.title: FX
pages.purpose: Delay, reverb, saturation, and send/master effects.

pages.title: Scenes
pages.purpose: Scene A/B morphing and performance snapshots.

pages.title: Banks
pages.purpose: Pattern banks, song organization, import/export, and MIDI export.

pages.title: Set
pages.purpose: Transport, MIDI, assistant, export, and system-level controls.

modules.summary: Project schema, defaults, serialization, and persistence.
Web Audio graph, tracks, voices, effects, transport-adjacent scheduling, and MIDI output.
Bootstrap, routing, scheduling, UI wiring, MIDI learn, and transport control.
Studio canvas, module layout, and workspace controls.
Keyboard context, live feedback, and page help strips.
Low-latency AudioWorklet processors for resampling and special synth engines.
Static file host, assistant API, and sample file serving.

renderingRules: Keep the assistant off the audio thread.
Treat realtime audio paths as deterministic and low-latency.
Prefer explicit state changes through the server or UI boundary.

persistence.assetStoreGoal: IndexedDB or filesystem-backed project assets for samples and recorder buffers
persistence.warning: Do not assume saved projects contain embedded audio assets unless the asset layer is explicitly implemented.

assistantGuardrails: Use the manual and provider catalog before proposing changes.
Prefer browser-first flows and explicit API boundaries.
Do not route AI logic through the audio thread.
When music playback is requested, suggest patterns, scenes, arrangement moves, or references rather than trying to synthesize audio in the assistant layer.

providerNotes: OpenAI uses the Responses API by default.
Local OpenAI-compatible providers can be pointed at LM Studio, local gateways, or other compatible endpoints.
Ollama support is available for its native chat endpoint.
